title: Tzatziki
subtitle: Greek yoghurt, cucumber, and garlic dip
excerpt: Tzatziki is a thick, cool dip of strained yoghurt, grated cucumber, garlic, and dill. It serves as a side sauce for grilled meats, a dip for bread and crudités, or part of a mezze spread. The key is squeezing as much moisture as possible from the cucumber before mixing.
sourceNotes: Tzatziki is a staple of Greek cooking and part of a broader family of yoghurt-cucumber dips found across Turkey and the Middle East. The Greek version uses thick strained yoghurt, dill, and olive oil.

recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make up to 24 hours ahead. The flavour improves as the garlic and dill infuse into the yoghurt.

content.text: Tzatziki is a Greek yoghurt dip with grated cucumber, garlic, dill, and olive oil. It is cold, thick, and sharp, and it pairs with almost anything cooked over a flame. The only technique involved is getting the moisture out of the cucumber; skip that step and the dip is watery inside twenty minutes.

Grate {{cucumber}} cucumber on the coarse side of a box grater. Tip into a clean tea towel, gather the ends, and twist to squeeze out as much liquid as possible. Alternatively, place in a sieve, sprinkle with a little salt, and leave for 15 minutes, then squeeze dry.
Combine the yoghurt, squeezed cucumber, {{garlic}} of crushed garlic, dill, {{extra-virgin-olive-oil}} of olive oil, and lemon juice in a bowl. Season with {{sea-salt-fine}} of salt. Stir well.
Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes before serving so the garlic mellows into the yoghurt. Drizzle with a little more olive oil and scatter a few fronds of dill over the top to serve.

items.symptom: The dip becomes watery as it sits
items.fix: Squeeze the grated cucumber again through a clean cloth. Do it twice if needed. The cucumber contains a lot of water and it will all end up in the dip otherwise.

items.cause: Garlic added too close to serving
items.fix: Make the dip at least an hour ahead. The garlic rounds out significantly with time in the yoghurt. For a milder flavour, use just 1 clove.

items.symptom: The dip is not thick enough
items.cause: Yoghurt was not strained or was a thin variety
items.fix: Use full-fat Greek yoghurt. If it still seems thin, hang it in a piece of muslin or a clean cloth over a bowl in the fridge for 30 minutes to drain off more whey.

content.text: Tzatziki appears on Greek tables as part of a mezze spread, alongside olives, feta, and bread. It is the natural companion to souvlaki and gyros. Outside Greece, it has become a widely used dip and a standard accompaniment to grilled meats across Europe. A bowl of it in the fridge keeps for two days.
